prompt:
A 3D-printed PLA generator housing designed to hold a small DC motor and a hand-crank mechanism, shown as a standalone part on a workbench. The housing should have clear functional geometry: mounting bosses, screw holes, a motor cradle, and a channel/exit for wires, with visible layer lines and a clean printed finish. Include a few nearby fasteners (nuts/bolts) and a detached shaft coupler or bushing to make it obvious this is a mechanical enclosure for a crank generator.

Ultra-detailed, photorealistic 512×512 image in a tidy near-future solarpunk workshop: soft cinematic lighting, realistic plastics and metal, no brands, no readable text, no UI overlays. Keep the composition simple and centered so the housing reads clearly as an inventory object.
